source.quote: Hong Kong Dog Rescue is a non-profit organization which is devoted to saving the lives of abandoned dogs.

source.quote: All of the dogs and puppies that HKDR take in for re-homing are health checked, vaccinated, given flea, tick and heartworm prevention and de-sexed. Those who require additional veterinary treatment or surgery are given the best available.

significant_events.summary: Rescuing abandoned dogs, providing health checks, vaccinations, de-sexing, and rehoming. Includes rehabilitation for behavioral issues.

source.quote: Alongside the rescue and re-homing efforts, education is also an important part of HKDR's mission, with workshops and online seminars on a variety of subjects being regularly hosted.

significant_events.summary: Hosting workshops and online seminars to educate the public, especially youth, on pet welfare and responsible ownership.

source.quote: HKDR relies heavily on the time and efforts of dedicated volunteers to be able to operate and many of the important functions of the organisation are handled solely by such volunteers, such as our Foster and Life Saver Club programmes, and even the Volunteer Coordination itself.

significant_events.summary: Operating foster and life saver club programs, relying on dedicated volunteers for important organizational functions.

source.quote: The fund-raising activity is an event organized by Hong Kong Dog Rescue (HKDR) under the Public Subscription Permit No. 2017/289/1 issued by the Social Welfare Department of the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region. The purposes of this activity are to raise funds for raise funds for on-going operation cost for the two Homing Centres (Tai Po & Ap Lei Chau), including vet fee, dog food, utilities and wages.

significant_events.summary: A charitable fund-raising event held on 19 November 2017 to support the ongoing operational costs of two Homing Centres.

data.original_text: HKDR was founded in 2003 for the specific purpose of saving dogs and puppies from the Hong Kong Government's Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department (AFCD) Animal Management Centres, where at that time thousands of dogs and puppies were destroyed every year.

These days most of the dogs and puppies taken in by HKDR come from a variety of other sources. Some of these dogs are ex-breeder dogs, those that are no longer useful for the purpose of producing puppies for the pet trade.

All of the dogs and puppies that HKDR take in for re-homing are health checked, vaccinated, given flea, tick and heartworm prevention and de-sexed. Those who require additional veterinary treatment or surgery are given the best available.
Dogs with behavioural problems are rehabilitated using Positive Reinforcement training methods to make them ready for adoption.

HKDR relies heavily on the time and efforts of dedicated volunteers to be able to operate and many of the important functions of the organisation are handled solely by such volunteers, such as our Foster and Life Saver Club programmes, and even the Volunteer Coordination itself.
Alongside the rescue and re-homing efforts, education is also an important part of HKDR's mission, with workshops and online seminars on a variety of subjects being regularly hosted.
Hong Kong Dog Rescue is a non-profit organization which is devoted to saving the lives of abandoned dogs.
Their mission is to 'Rescue, Rehabilitate and Rehome' as said on their website.
HKDR has cared for and re-homed more than 10,000 dogs while being largely funded by private donations, sales of HKDR merchandises and fundraising events.
Total rehomed ~11000 dogs since established in 2003.

Up to 2022, HKDR outreached over 120,000 students through almost 50 educational talks, workshops, group visits, public events and school projects to educate the younger generation about pet welfare, adoption and responsible pet ownership.
